item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How has this gotten no comments in almost SEVEN years? This is neat! Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. My personal headcanon for this one is that the egg was responsible for the state of the Earth back then to begin with, and was accidentally knocked over, which caused the extinction event.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit of a shame it comes right after another dinosaurs one, and also a terraforming one, but this is very cool nonetheless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted due to Dinosaurs done is a suitably disconcerting way (Who built it? Why? Is it/Are they active hostile to current life? What happens if it starts spawning diseases?) The idea that people would try to crack it open is a -BIT- LolFoundation, but I think the tone of how it was presented it suitably worded enough to not cross the line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted despite an addendum that borders LolFoundation, because the rest of it is very good and thought provoking.

Well met.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I have to wonder, why is it assumed that this device is designed to imitate a naturally-occurring period in Earth history? It seems much more interesting to theorize that perhaps the entire period in question was actually created by the device, which is now attempting to continue doing so. Perhaps the extinction event was related to (or even identical to) the burial of the device. Or, even stranger and more in keeping with the inexplicable feel of a good SCP, maybe the entire period and the device's continued attempts are merely a byproduct of an altogether more sinister purpose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The note at the end is preventing me from upvoting. Of course that's not allowed, cracking SCPs open to see what's inside would be completely idiotic. They might as well ask if they can combine it with other SCPs to see what happens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. There's answers to everything on this site!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel as though my childhood love of dinosaurs is being pandered to. Hard. And somehow I don't care.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would dinosaurs be able to survive with the present-day bacterial flora?

No.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a cool story. Does it also create microorganisms? I always wondered, if we could create dinosaurs, whether they would be able to survive with the present-day bacterial flora.

They're not dinosaurs. They're vintage lifeforms.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe, or maybe they would say "Hey, where are my dinosaurs? Is this thing working? My wife really liked Lystrosaurus! Oh well, let's crank this sucker up to 11".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I kept seeing Lysistrata. Something's weird with my brain parts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: gotta say, I'm not too great at figuring out what/where the inspiration for a whole lot of SCPs come from, so I've got to use the discussion page to figure it out. I like this. It's not trying to kill anyone, not harmful (well, at it's current capacity), but there are probably a whole lot more from where that came from (ah, headcanon, I love you so). upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like it. I think it could use more weirdness, but I also think enough is here to make it work. +1'ed

(Also, for those of you who don't know, Lystrosaurus was the sole dominant species on Earth for hundreds of millions of years. Just… just imagine a vast sea of chihuahua-sized lizard-hippos. That's the world of Lystrosaurus.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Aaaand you have now given me an idea for an SCP/Anomalous object.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They'd be all "huh, would you look at that. Well, it's been 250 million years, the planet's been through multiple mass extinctions in that time, so it's no surprise that the dominant technological lifeform is something we had no idea about. Let's take some for sampling so we can analyze their genomes! I wonder if they're descended from anything we collected last time?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I imagine that if we crack it open, it'll be like a pinata. Except instead of candy we get dinosaurs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what the creatures who made this would think if they came back and found out some hairless monkeys have been messing with their machine? Hmmm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this, novoted, came back and read it again, and am now upvoting. A couple of things kept me on the fence:

It might help this article to hint at the existence of other such eggs. Who/whatever made this egg would have to know just one egg would be unlikely to repopulate the entire planet, leading me to believe there would be others (your skip though, so tell me if I am incorrect).

Again, I am basing this on my perception of the creator(s) of this object, but something besides popping organisms into existence might happen, to aid in distribution of these things. Maybe it affects local weather patterns to increase rainfall and cause high winds to aid in seed dispersal? Maybe it causes some sort of reproductive/and or nomadic compulsions in reptilian/avian lifeforms?

Overall I liked the concept enough to upvote, but I just feel like a thing capable of making this object would put more sophisticated means in place to make it successful in its implied purpose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noted and fixed. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. I'm a sucker for terraforming. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One small nitpick:

Attempts to date the artifact have met with only limited success as all attempts to sample its surface material have failed to date.

This would read better if the "to date" at the end was removed because it sounds redundant. You use the words "to date" frequently in the article. "Thus far", "Since acquisition", "Prior", would work in certain places where you re-used "to date".

Still gets a +1 from me though because somebody else remembered the Permian-Triassic extinction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some scientific background for the uninitiated:

The Permian-Triassic extinction event, known among some in the paleontological circle as the "Great Dying", was the most devastating extinction event in Earth's history. Far more potent than the more famous Cretaceous-Paleogene event 65 million years ago, the Great Dying resulted in the extinction of approximately 90 to 96% of all species alive during that time, a catastrophe of such proportions that it took ten million years for the Earth to recover. And unlike the death of the dinosaurs, there's no convenient smoking gun to point at to suggest exactly what caused it; 252 million years wipes out pretty much any kind of evidence that we could go on.

All we know is that something was aware of what was going on and actively trying to save as much life as possible when it happened. Whether it was Mother Earth herself or an observant alien intelligence is unknown; for all we know this event was intentionally caused and the ark a mere afterthought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's neat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thought: This pairs with Mack's one chemical SCP what hates people and industrialization. They're both manifestations of the Earth's biosphere attempting to prevent or reverse mass extinctions, with varying degrees of success. Maybe this happens every time things go to shit around here, and some day we'll find something that turns people into trilobites, and one that creates dinosaurs out of the materials around it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the draft pretty well, and I like the finished version a bit more. The idea that someone was terraforming way back then is excellent. The fact that we have no idea who or what they were is… moreso.

Be a lot of fun to see a tale based on this. Have something properly sapient walk out.
